title: anemone pulsatilla subsp. gotlandica
intro: Anemone pulsatilla subsp. gotlandica, also known as the Gotland anemone, is a subspecies of the anemone plant, specifically a flowering plant native to the island of Gotland in Sweden. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic, anticonvulsant, and antihistaminic effects. The health benefits associated with Anemone pulsatilla subsp. gotlandica include relief from menstrual cramps, anxiety, and insomnia, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and fever. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential interactions with medications and side effects.
